title: Search on task description fields is 10-15s slower since v2.3 upgrade (~5k tasks)
problem: Searching for keywords that match task description bodies takes 10-15 seconds to return results, while searches matching task titles return almost instantly. The reporter has ~5,000 tasks and the slowdown began around the time they upgraded from TaskFlow 2.2 to 2.3 approximately two weeks ago.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3 upgrade likely introduced a regression in how task description fields are indexed or queried. Possible causes: a full-text index on the description column was dropped or not migrated during the upgrade, the search query plan changed to a sequential scan on descriptions while titles retain an index, or a new search implementation in v2.3 handles description fields differently (e.g., dropped an index, changed to unoptimized LIKE/ILIKE queries, or broke an existing full-text search configuration).
reproduction_steps: Create or use an account with a large number of tasks (~5,000) that have text in both title and description fields
Open the main search bar in TaskFlow 2.3
Search for a keyword (e.g., 'budget') that exists only in task descriptions, not titles
Observe that results take 10-15 seconds to return
Search for a keyword that exists in a task title
Observe that results return almost instantly
environment: Ubuntu 22.04, ThinkPad T14, 32GB RAM, TaskFlow v2.3 (upgraded from v2.2 ~2 weeks ago), ~5,000 tasks
impact: Any user with a significant number of tasks experiences severely degraded search performance when searching task descriptions — a core workflow. This is a regression from v2.3 affecting usability for power users.
recommended_fix: Compare the database schema and query plan for description search between v2.2 and v2.3. Check whether a full-text index on the task description column was dropped or altered in the v2.3 migration. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the search query with a description match vs. a title match to confirm the difference. If an index was dropped, restore it; if the query changed, revert or optimize the description search path.
proposed_test_case: Create a test with 5,000+ tasks with known keywords in descriptions. Assert that a keyword search matching description text completes within an acceptable threshold (e.g., under 2 seconds). Run this test against both v2.2 and v2.3 schemas to confirm the regression and validate the fix.
information_gaps: No server-side logs or query timing data to confirm whether the bottleneck is database, application, or network
Unknown whether other v2.3 users with large task counts experience the same issue
No information on database engine or configuration (PostgreSQL, SQLite, etc.)
